titleStmt.title:
The Tibetan Cabinet Ministers to President Truman
A similar message sent to the Secretary of State, not printed.

head.hi: The Tibetan Cabinet Ministers to President Truman
head.note: A similar message sent to the Secretary of State, not printed.

div.p: Sir: For the welfare of Tibet, the Financial Secretary of the Government of Tibet, Tsepon Shakabpa, with his assistant Khenchung Changkhimpa have been sent to observe the trade conditions regarding imports from India, America, China, and England, and export of wool, Yak’s tail, and fur skins from Tibet and also to purchase gold and silver.
Kindly recognise them as our trade mission and necessary help in their discussion on trade matters and in purchasing gold and silver, considering the good relation between America and Tibet.

closer.dateline: With our customary greeting scarf, dated 26th of the 8th month of Tibetan Fire-Pig year [1947].
